ai_analysis.impact: An attacker can execute arbitrary system commands on the target system via the vsFTPd 2.3.4 backdoor.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Update vsFTPd to version 3.x to remediate this vulnerability.

ai_analysis.impact: An attacker can gain remote code execution (RCE) on the target system via OpenSSH due to the outdated version.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Update OpenSSH to the latest version, specifically OpenSSH 9.4p1 or later.

findings.solution: Update OpenSSL to version 1.0.2 or later

ai_analysis.impact: An attacker can potentially decrypt SSL/TLS sessions and obtain sensitive information, including authentication credentials and encrypted data.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Update OpenSSL to version 1.0.2 or later to mitigate the SSL POODLE vulnerability (CVE-2014-3566).

findings.nvt_name: Anonymous Diffie-Hellman Key Exchange MitM Vulnerability
findings.solution: Update OpenSSL to version 1.0.2 or later

ai_analysis.impact: An attacker can intercept and manipulate encrypted communications, potentially leading to full compromise of the target system.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Update OpenSSL to version 1.0.2 or later to mitigate this vulnerability.

ai_analysis.impact: An attacker can execute arbitrary code on the target system via a specially crafted FTP request, resulting in full compromise.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Update vsFTPd to version 3.x or later to address the vulnerability CVE-2011-2523.

ai_analysis.impact: An attacker can execute arbitrary code on the Bind 9.4.2 server, leading to full compromise.
An attacker can exploit this vulnerability to cause a denial-of-service (DoS) or potentially execute arbitrary code on the Bind 9.4.2 server.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Update Bind to version 9.11.5 or later to fix CVE-2020-8616 and CVE-2016-1286.

ai_analysis.impact: An attacker can execute arbitrary code on the Bind 9.4.2 server, potentially leading to full compromise of the system.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Update Bind to version 9.11.5 or later to fix the vulnerability (CNVD-2020-34454).

ai_analysis.impact: An attacker can perform a Slowloris DOS attack, potentially leading to a denial of service (DoS) condition on the Apache server.
ai_analysis.recommendation: Update Apache to the latest version, specifically 2.4.54 or later, to address this vulnerability.
